pattern: Tracking post order globally and merging each followed user's most-recent tweets with a heap yields the 10 most recent news feed items in O(k log k) where k is the number of followees.
hints: Each tweet needs a global timestamp so you can compare recency across different users without storing an absolute clock.
A user's news feed is the 10 most recent tweets from themselves and everyone they follow — gather those candidate lists and merge them by recency.
A min-heap of size 10 (or a merge of sorted per-user tweet lists) keeps the top-10 extraction efficient.

edge_inputs.description: standard sequence: post, read own feed, follow, post, read merged feed, unfollow, read own feed
news feed capped at 10 when more than 10 tweets exist
getNewsFeed on a user who has never posted and follows nobody returns empty list
a user always sees their own tweets without explicitly following themselves
unfollow a user you never followed is a no-op and subsequent feed is unaffected
feed after follow correctly interleaves tweets from both users by recency
following yourself is a no-op — duplicate tweets must not appear in feed

stress.description: randomized sequence of posts, follows, unfollows, and feed reads over many users
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['Twitter']
    args = [[]]
    tweet_id = [0]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r = rng.random()
        u = rng.randint(1, 10)
        if r < 0.35:
            tweet_id[0] += 1
            ops.append('postTweet')
            args.append([u, tweet_id[0]])
        elif r < 0.55:
            ops.append('getNewsFeed')
            args.append([u])
        elif r < 0.75:
            v = rng.randint(1, 10)
            ops.append('follow')
            args.append([u, v])
        else:
            v = rng.randint(1, 10)
            ops.append('unfollow')
            args.append([u, v])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: small user pool (1-10) causes many follow/unfollow cycles and feed merges; tweet_id always unique and increasing

complexity.time: O(k log k) per getNewsFeed where k is followee count; O(1) for other ops
